nodes.body: <<FILL role=setup words=120 beats='Nia Reyes, eleven, stands in the rain-washed dusk before the Star-Wagon Hotel, a hundred dark windows and one grand door; her family inherited it yesterday from Uncle Solomon, its last keeper, and it has been closed for forty years; Mom and Dad are downstairs in the office arguing happily with paperwork, so Nia is the one holding the brass ring of one hundred numbered keys and the candle lantern; the hotel does not feel empty, it feels like a held breath; she decides someone should say hello to it properly, tonight'>>

choices.label: Lift the big key, unlock the grand door, and step inside.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=setup words=110 beats='the lobby by lantern light, marble and dust sheets, a ring of hanging lanterns asleep in canvas, a staircase wide enough for a parade; Nia's footsteps wake little echoes that seem pleased about it; and at the far end, impossibly, one small lamp is burning at the long front desk, polished amid the dust, as if the hotel kept one corner ready all these years; warm curiosity, a delicious shiver, never dread'>>

choices.label: Walk toward the one lit lamp at the front desk.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=setup words=128 beats='behind the desk stands a small silver-haired woman Nia can see the wallpaper through, straightening a register with see-through hands; she is Bibi, and she is kind and glad and utterly unsurprising about being a ghost; she explains gently: her family built and kept the Star-Wagon, five of them in all, and on the night of the hotel's centennial spectacular a great storm shut everything down and the evening was never finished; in the forty quiet years since, her family has faded apart across the floors and forgotten one another, even their names, even her; she keeps the desk so the lights stay on for them; she asks Nia for one great favor, find them, and help finish the evening at last, one grand night in the big top'>>

choices.label: Promise her, take the keys, and climb to the grand staircase landing.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=108 beats='the grand staircase landing, the crossroads of the whole Star-Wagon; from here the hotel spreads out like an opened hand: below, the lamplit box office wagon behind the front desk; along one corridor, the double doors of the mess wagon breathing a faint warm smell; behind frosted glass, the green glow of the menagerie wagon; above, the sleeper cars where something quick is laughing; higher still, the narrow stair to the observation caboose; and straight across, the shut grand doors of the big top itself; keep this scene general so it reads naturally on a first visit or on any return trip between wings'>>

choices.label: Go back down into the box office wagon, where Bibi keeps the desk.
Follow the warm bread smell to the mess wagon.
Push through the frosted doors of the menagerie wagon.
Climb to the sleeper cars and that quick laughter.
Take the narrow stair up toward the observation caboose.
Cross to the grand big top doors.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=96 beats='the box office wagon by lantern light, seen from the foot of the stairs: dust sheets draped like sleeping swans, tall windows striped with moonlight, and the long front desk with its one faithful lamp where Bibi keeps her post; behind the counter a doorway shows a wall of pigeonhole mailboxes, and a side door stands ajar on a room of brass sockets and cords; keep this arrival general so it reads naturally on a first visit or any return'>>

choices.label: Go to Bibi at the glowing desk.
Slip behind the counter to the ticket booth cubby.
Ease open the side door to the old old telephone exchange car.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='Bibi at her desk, polishing the silver call bell in small patient circles; she brightens at Nia's company like a lamp turned up; write her welcome warm and general, the kind that works the same whether this is Nia's first stop after the staircase or the fifth time tonight she has drifted back to the desk; behind Bibi a wall of numbered wagon keys gleams, every key polished though no guest has come in forty years'>>

choices.label: Ask Bibi about her family.
Ask about the hotel's greatest night.
Ask about the wall of gleaming keys.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=104 beats='Bibi tries to remember her family and comes back with everything except the people: someone who smelled of bread and thyme, someone who hummed to plants, two small someones who were never not running; the names and faces slide away like water off glass, and her lamp dims a shade before she straightens and brightens it herself; everything the Star-Wagon ever knew is written down somewhere, she says, that is what a hotel is, a building that keeps track of its people'>>

choices.label: Search the ticket booth cubby for what the hotel kept.
Try the ringmaster's office wagon behind the desk.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='Bibi lights up telling the preparations for the centennial spectacular: a frosted centennial cake taller than the two smallest members of staff, gardenia buttonholes ordered by the hundred, a brand-new waltz written just for the night; then she reaches for who the frosted centennial cake was shaped like and who wrote the waltz, and the memory slips, and she laughs at herself sadly, a hostess who has mislaid her own party'>>

choices.label: Ask gently what happened to the ball.
Go look for the reservation book in the ringmaster's office wagon.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=102 beats='the storm, told the way Bibi remembers it, in sounds: rain like gravel on the tall windows, the lights failing between one dance and the next, guests laughing at first and then hurrying, doors shutting one hundred times; we always meant to finish it, she says, we kept saying soon; write her telling entry-neutral, the same story fresh on a first hearing or turned over deeper on a return, never a one-time confession'>>

choices.label: Look for the ball's reservation book in the office.
Check what the ticket booth cubby still holds from that night.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=96 beats='Bibi walks Nia down the wall of numbered wagon keys like a captain showing her ship: every key polished every night, room by room, because habits are how you love a building; her hand hovers over hook 100 at the end, a key smaller and brighter than the rest, and for a moment she cannot say why polishing that one always takes the longest'>>

choices.label: Ask her about the family the keys were polished for.
Slip into the ringmaster's office wagon behind the desk.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=98 beats='the ticket booth cubby: one hundred pigeonholes floor to ceiling, most empty, a few still holding shapes that make Nia's lantern hand slow down; box 9 has a yellowed envelope that smells faintly of thyme; box 44 bristles with folded folded paper streamers; and the top row, up a collapsible step-ladder, keeps its secrets in shadow'>>

choices.label: Open box 9 and the thyme-smelling envelope.
Unfold the folded paper streamers in box 44.
Roll the ladder over and climb toward the top row.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='the envelope holds a old show program in careful cursive: seven courses for the centennial spectacular, each dish named like a member of an orchestra, and at the bottom, underlined twice, FOR Bibi'S centennial spectacular; whoever cooked for this hotel was not staff only, he was somebody's son, and the kitchen was where he said so'>>

choices.label: Carry the old show program to Bibi at the desk.
Check the old telephone exchange car too.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='the darts are folded from luggage tags, creased sharp by practiced small fingers, and tucked behind them is a juggling score card in two different scrawls, M VS M, BEST OF 1000, GAME PAUSED; the pause has lasted a very long time; Nia smooths one dart flat and folds it back better than she found it'>>

choices.label: Take a dart and the juggling score card to Bibi.
Check the old telephone exchange car too.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=92 beats='the collapsible step-ladder grumbles along its rail; the top row of pigeonholes sits in forty years of soft gray dust, all except one box whose front is wiped clean in a neat circle, as if someone small once polished a porthole to look through; the ladder wobbles cheerfully under Nia's sneakers, opinionated but willing'>>

choices.label: Steady the ladder and open the clean-circled box.
Stretch on tiptoe for the far corner box instead.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='inside lies a flat box lined with tissue: one pressed circus rosette, still faintly sweet, and a note in a round green-inked hand, FOR THE BUTTONHOLES, FROM THE WINTER GARDEN, WITH LOVE, W; the flower is forty years old and looks ready to be worn tonight; somebody grew things for this family the way other people write letters'>>

choices.label: Bring the pressed circus rosette down to Bibi.
Look into the old telephone exchange car as well.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=98 beats='the old telephone exchange car: a wall of brass sockets and cloth-wrapped cords, a headset on a hook, and a brass megaphone like a great ear trumpet aimed at the whole hotel; one socket, room by room long dead, still carries a faint furry buzz, a line that never quite hung up; Nia's fingers itch toward the cords'>>

choices.label: Plug the headset into the buzzing line.
Page the whole hotel through the brass megaphone.
Leave it and try the ringmaster's office wagon.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='the line opens like a seashell held to her ear: not voices but the hotel itself, faraway and alive, a whisk ticking a bowl, a self-watering can filling, quick feet on carpet, the slow creak of something dome-shaped turning; every sound a floor, every floor still busy in its faded way; Nia holds her breath so as not to interrupt'>>

choices.label: Keep listening, sound by sound, and map them.
Speak into the line.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='she sorts the sounds like a detective: the whisk lives below the warm smell, the self-watering can behind the frosted glass, the running feet own the numbered floors, the creak turns under the stars; four corners, four faded someones keeping their old posts, exactly as Bibi said; the hotel has been holding its family all along'>>

choices.label: Go tell Bibi what the line just told you.
Head back to the staircase landing.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='Nia leans to the mouthpiece and says the truest thing she has: Bibi is at the desk, and she misses you; the buzz goes very still, the whole line listening; then, faint as a music box under a pillow, the line hums three bars of a waltz back to her before settling into a warmer kind of quiet; message received, somewhere'>>

choices.label: Tell Bibi what the line hummed.
Slip back to the staircase landing.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=98 beats='Nia clears her throat and pages the Star-Wagon: good evening, this is the front desk, Bibi is here and the lights are on; her voice rolls away down a hundred corridors and fades; a long hush; then, far and faint, four answers come one after another, a pot lid clashed like a cymbal, a tap-tap on tin, two knocks in perfect unison, a whistle down a stairwell; heard, heard, heard, and heard'>>

choices.label: Run and tell Bibi the whole hotel answered.
Wait right here by the horn for someone to come down.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='the ringmaster's office wagon behind the desk: a rolltop desk with a stubborn latch, a framed troupe photograph turned to face the wall, a brass show clock stopped and dusty, and a lower drawer packed with letters in rubber bands gone brittle; the room smells of ink and quiet; everything in it seems to be waiting to be asked a question'>>

choices.label: Turn the portrait around.
Work the rolltop desk open.
Slip the rubber band off the bundle of old postcards.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=98 beats='the framed troupe photograph shows five figures on the hotel's front steps on some bright opening day, but time has softened every face to a warm blur, as if even paint can fade apart; the shapes still tell their story, a tall figure in cook's whites, one in gardening gloves and a sun hat, two small ones in bellhop uniforms mid-fidget, and at the center a woman with a little silver bell in her painted hand'>>

choices.label: Open the rolltop and hunt for their names.
Carry the portrait out to Bibi.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='the latch gives and the rolltop rattles up over the one book Nia has been hoping for, the booking ledger for the centennial spectacular; the guest list opens flat, and the top five lines are written in Bibi's own copperplate, Leon, FAMILY; Marisol, FAMILY; Coco, FAMILY; Jojo, FAMILY; and last, smaller, Bibi, FAMILY FIRST, GUESTS AFTER; five names, safe in ink all along'>>

choices.label: Read the five names aloud, right here.
Carry the ledger straight to Bibi.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=94 beats='Leon, Marisol, Coco, Jojo, Bibi; Nia reads them slowly, like ringing five small bells, and the office listens; the lamp flame stands taller, dust motes swirl up in a slow glad spiral like confetti practicing, and somewhere out in the lobby the desk bell gives one bright unstruck ding; on the mantel the stopped dusty clock seems to lean toward the sound, wanting to join in; names have weight in this hotel'>>

choices.label: Bring the ledger and the names to Bibi.
First, wind the stopped brass show clock back to life.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=94 beats='the brass show clock winds sweetly and starts with a tick like a heartbeat found; when it chimes the hour, other clocks answer from far rooms one by one, late by seconds, like sleepy voices joining a song, until the whole dark hotel is agreeing softly about the time; a building synchronizing itself around one small act of care'>>

choices.label: Follow the chiming upstairs, clock to clock.
Go show Bibi what you have found tonight.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=104 beats='Nia spreads tonight's lobby findings across the desk blotter; write this general, whatever she happens to carry this trip, a menu, a paper dart, a pressed flower, a ledger, or only news, so it reads true on any path; Bibi touches each find or each word like a keeper checking room by room, and her lamp burns warmer with every one; the hotel kept track of us, she whispers, I knew it would; her eyes come up bright and resolved'>>

choices.label: Ask her to tell the rest, now that it is closer.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=106 beats='Bibi's story, told steady and warm, entry-neutral, the full truth fresh on a first hearing or the same truth going deeper on a return: the storm that stopped the ball, the years the family kept the hotel anyway, the slow fading that came after the doors closed, each of them drifting to the floor they loved best until even names wore thin; and the desk, kept so anyone finding their way home would see the lights on; it is not the hotel that needs reopening, she finishes, it is the evening that needs finishing'>>

choices.label: Promise her the evening, properly this time.
Ask what waits behind door 100 first.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='door 100, Bibi says, and her voice goes soft as flannel: the family suite at the top of the house, the one door we kept for ourselves; the plan was always to end the centennial spectacular up there, all five of us on its balcony over the town; she polishes its small bright key without looking, the habit older than the forgetting'>>

choices.label: Promise her the evening, balcony and all.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='Nia gives her word as the new keeper of the hundred keys, and Bibi stands straighter than lamplight, sets her cap, and begins polishing the silver bell for the big night; write the parting general so it reads the same however Nia got here; the grand staircase waits through the archway, and across the landing the big top doors wait for someone with a key and a promise'>>

choices.label: Head back to the grand staircase landing.
Go straight to the big top and begin.
Stay a while and sort the hundred keys with Bibi.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=ending words=96 beats='the ladder has opinions about tiptoes; it glides grandly sideways with Nia aboard like a slow parade float, and she rides it all the way to the wall with a squawk, upsetting three pigeonholes of ancient postcards on the way; no bruises, only a blizzard of forty-year-old mail to gather by lantern light, and by the time the last card is boxed the windows are going gray; Bibi walks her to the door, ruffles her hair, and calls her the best help in decades; tomorrow night, the top row, with both hands on the rungs'>>

<<FILL role=ending words=94 beats='Nia waits by the horn, chin on her fists, listening to the hotel settle; the answers do not come down, not tonight, faded feet are slow feet, and the big carpeted dark between floors is a long way for a thin memory to travel; she wakes at dawn in the switchboard chair under a blanket she does not remember, the headset hung neatly back on its hook, and a peppermint on the ledger beside her; not a failure, a first hello; next time she will bring the party to them'>>

<<FILL role=ending words=98 beats='Nia follows the chiming up through the dark, winding every clock she finds, the grandfather on the mezzanine, the little enamel clock in the card room, the drum-bellied kitchen timer that makes her jump; by dawn the Star-Wagon ticks in one voice for the first time in forty years, and Nia knows the whole building the way you know a song; the party is not tonight, but a hotel that agrees about the time is a hotel getting ready; she sleeps till noon and dreams in ticks and chimes'>>

ending.title: The Hotel Agrees About the Time

nodes.body: <<FILL role=ending words=96 beats='the letters are Uncle Solomon's, one a year for forty years, all addressed simply TO THE Star-Wagon: news of the town, pressed flowers, apologies for the long closing, and in every single one the same closing line, KEEP THEM COMPANY TILL SOMEONE YOUNGER COMES; Nia reads by lantern until the sky pinks, understanding at last that she was not an accident of paperwork, she was expected; she walks home holding the last letter like a ticket to something that has already begun'>>

<<FILL role=ending words=98 beats='they sort the hundred keys together, and every key turns out to be a story Bibi can still tell, the honeymooners of room 12, the opera singer in 61 who practiced into the wardrobe, the dog that was smuggled into 27 inside a hatbox and smuggled out three sizes bigger; the stories last until the windows silver, and when Nia finally yawns her way home the ring in her pocket no longer feels like metal, it feels like a book; the party will keep for one more night, the stories nearly did not'>>

ending.title: One Hundred Keys, One Hundred Stories

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=98 beats='the kitchen's double doors swing at a touch, and the impossible smell arrives first, warm bread and thyme in a hotel closed forty years; beyond the doors a corridor splits three ways, toward the great main kitchen where copper glints, down a narrow pantry passage lined with shelves, and toward a bakery where an oven ticks with heat; keep this arrival general so it reads naturally on a first visit or any return'>>

choices.label: Push into the great main kitchen.
Take the narrow pantry passage.
Follow the bread smell to the bakery.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=104 beats='the great kitchen: a city of copper pots, a stove glowing with ghost-light, and at its heart a broad transparent man in cook's whites, tasting from a long spoon and humming; write him general and re-entry-safe, a cook mid-shift whether Nia is seeing him for the first time or the third, he takes a midnight visitor in easy stride, mess wagon are never closed to hungry children; steam that is not quite steam curls up through him and he salts it anyway'>>

choices.label: Say hello politely from the doorway.
Watch him work a while first.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=96 beats='the cook beams like an oven door opening, waves Nia onto a stool at the worktable, and is three sentences into the secret of proper soup before she has sat down; he talks recipes the way other people talk about their families, which may be exactly what he is doing; midway he reaches for the name of whoever taught him the thyme trick, and the sentence just, softly, runs out'>>

choices.label: Sit and try the soup he ladles out.
Ask who taught him the thyme trick.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=94 beats='the soup steams like real soup and tastes like memory, warm and buttery and somehow exactly like the best birthday Nia ever had; she says so and the cook goes pink with pride clear through; food remembers, he says, tapping his nose, even when cooks forget; the word forget lands on the table between them, quiet as a dropped napkin'>>

choices.label: Ask who taught him to cook like this.
Ask who all this cooking is for.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='from the doorway Nia watches him plate seven beautiful courses, carry them to the long crew mess table, set them down just so, wait with his hands folded, sigh, clear them, and begin again; it is the saddest, kindest loop she has ever seen, a meal kept ready for people who never come; on the order rail above the pass one yellowed supper chit flutters, and the crew mess table's five chairs sit in a row'>>

choices.label: Step in and say hello.
Quietly read the yellowed supper chit on the rail.
Look closer at the five chairs at the crew mess table.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=94 beats='five places, set perfectly; five chairs, each worn its own way, two nicked low down by swinging feet, one dusted forever with flour, one with a folded gardening glove left over the back like a bookmark, and one tall stool at the head with a cushion flattened by years of somebody small and upright; a long banquet table with the family missing, and the cook still setting it'>>

choices.label: Go say hello to the cook who sets it.
Read the yellowed supper chit on the order rail.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='the ticket, in copperplate Nia has seen the like of at the front desk: SEVEN COURSES FOR THE centennial spectacular, FAMILY TABLE FIRST, and a single initial, P; the order that was never cancelled, so the kitchen never stopped; forty years of soup kept warm because nobody rang down to say otherwise'>>

choices.label: Show the ticket to the cook.
Slip out to the staircase landing to think.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=96 beats='he circles the question like a pot he is afraid to burn: someone with flour on her sleeves and a little silver bell always at her elbow, someone who ran this whole house and still baked on Sundays; my mother, he says, wondering, I had a mother, in this very building, didn't I; his hat flickers brighter for a breath, then steadies'>>

choices.label: Ask who the seven courses are for.
Offer to help him cook while he thinks.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=96 beats='for the long banquet table, he says at once, then stops, ladle in the air; whoever they are; the order never came down to cancel, you see, so I keep it warm; forty years, he agrees, is a long time to keep a meal warm, but you do not stop cooking for your people just because you have mislaid them; Nia's chest aches in the good sad way'>>

choices.label: Offer to help him cook.
Go find the original yellowed supper chit on the rail.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='aprons on, and Nia is stirring while the cook narrates like a kindly storm; and a strange thing happens as they work: the busier his hands, the closer his memory creeps; the bread smell, he murmurs, my mother baked on Sundays even with a full brigade downstairs, and her name, her name was right there on the tip of the ladle; write this beat re-entry-safe, a memory circling nearer, never a one-time confession'>>

choices.label: Tell him the lady at the front desk is called Bibi.
Stay quiet and let him chase the memory himself.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='Bibi; the name goes through the kitchen like a struck bell, every copper pot ringing faintly in answer; write this re-entry-safe, the name striking fresh on a first telling and ringing deeper on a return, never a one-time revelation; the cook grips the worktable, eyes wide, whispering it twice, and for a moment he is bright as noon; he unties his apron to go to her, and falters at the doors, faded too thin to cross the dark alone; cooks come when dinner is called, he says finally, call a proper dinner, small guest, and I will come to her through any dark in this house'>>

choices.label: Promise him the call, and a real dinner to answer.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='Nia holds her tongue and stirs; the cook works the memory like dough, thyme, the thyme came from under glass, from the garden, from, and his face opens like a window, my sister grew the thyme, I have a sister, a sister with a sun hat and opinions about my parsley; he laughs out loud at the ceiling, one name closer to whole, and the stove burns brighter for it'>>

choices.label: Now tell him about the lady at the front desk too.
Let that be enough for tonight, and make him a promise.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='he holds the ticket the way you hold a photograph, and reads the initial with his thumb; P, he says, P rang the bell at the desk, P kept the whole house in her apron pocket, P is, and the word Bibi arrives before the name does, which is somehow better; he presses the ticket flat over his heart, then pins it back on the rail, top of the queue, where an order about to be filled belongs'>>

choices.label: Promise him the centennial spectacular will finally be called.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=96 beats='the pantry passage: shelves to the ceiling labeled in three different handwritings, copperplate, a round green scrawl, and two matching squiggles that fight for space on the same labels; at the end, a cold-room door wears a coat of frost though no machine has run in decades, and a ladder leans toward the high preserve shelves'>>

choices.label: Open the frosted cold-room door.
Climb toward the high preserve shelves.
Follow the passage on to the bakery.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=98 beats='inside the cold room, glowing faintly with its own winter, stands the frosted centennial cake; five tiers, sugar gardenias, Star-Wagon 100 in silver script, candles set but never lit, kept perfect all this time by frost and stubborn love; on the middle tier stand five small sugar acrobat figures, a cook, a gardener in a sun hat, two tiny bellhops, and a woman with a speck of a silver bell'>>

choices.label: Lean close and study the five sugar acrobat figures.
Go find the cook and ask about the frosted centennial cake.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=96 beats='Nia memorizes the sugar family: the cook's proud chest, the gardener's tipped hat, the twins caught mid-shove, the small upright woman with the bell; whoever iced this frosted centennial cake knew these five by heart and put that heart on the middle tier where everyone would see; a portrait in sugar, waiting forty years to be cut and shared'>>

choices.label: Bring the cook to see his own frosted centennial cake.
Stay and sketch each figure by lantern light.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='the cook stands before the frosted centennial cake with his hat pressed to his chest; write this re-entry-safe, awe that reads the same on any visit; I made this, he says, I remember the icing, three nights, and the little figures last of all; he touches the sugar woman's bell with one enormous gentle finger; five of us, he counts, wonder growing, five, and the candles never lit; well; a frosted centennial cake this patient deserves its party, and a cook who remembers how many he loves is halfway to remembering who'>>

choices.label: Promise him the candles will be lit.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=92 beats='the high shelves hold ranked preserve jars, and a row labeled in the round green hand, W'S PLUMS, TRADE FOR PASTRY, AS ALWAYS; a barter older than Nia, plums down from some garden, pastry up in exchange, a family economy still visible in glass and paper; one jar sits out of rank, lid loose, as if someone meant to check it'>>

choices.label: Leave the jars and open the cold room.
Reach up and check the pickled-plum jar.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=94 beats='the bakery: an oven warm with no fire, dough rising under a cloth like a slow breath, flour handprints on the table edge, and floury footprints crossing the tiles; on the worktable a loaf is braided in a braided sourdough loaf, and carved neat into the table's edge, ONE STRAND FOR EACH OF US; the room smells like Sunday morning in the middle of the night'>>

choices.label: Peek inside the warm oven.
Follow the floury footprints.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=94 beats='inside, a tray of golden funnel cakes shaped like stars, golden, fragrant, and never quite finished, because you do not serve the party bread before the party; the oven has been holding them at almost-ready for forty years, the most patient oven in the world; from deeper in the mess wagon drifts a hummed tune, three-four time, kept low like a secret'>>

choices.label: Go meet whoever bakes at midnight.
Follow the humming instead.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=96 beats='the humming is the cook's, a waltz worked into his kneading, push and turn and fold in three-four time; caught at it, he chuckles; my mother's waltz, he says, she wound the wind-up phonograph every, and he frowns at his floury hands, every what, every when; the tune knows its occasion even if he cannot name it; write him re-entry-safe, the same warm puzzlement on any visit'>>

choices.label: Tell him the lady at the desk is called Bibi.
Hum along and let the memory circle closer.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=92 beats='the footprints loop the worktable, pause at the braided sourdough loaf, and end at a little brass door in the wall, a small supply hoist, which chooses that moment to rattle politely, arriving; the mess wagon, Nia is learning, are never empty and never still, only quiet, the way a heart is quiet between beats'>>

choices.label: Head for the main kitchen and its cook.
Open the little brass small supply hoist door.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=96 beats='the small supply hoist holds a tray: one cocoa, still steaming, and a folded note, FOR THE SMALL GUEST, UP LATE; the hotel knows she is here, and the kitchen has opinions about children and bedtime; the little lift waits, rope creaking gently, big enough, just barely, for a curious eleven-year-old with poor impulse control'>>

choices.label: Send a thank-you note back on the tray.
Drink the cocoa right here, every warm drop.
Climb in and ride the small supply hoist.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='Nia prints her best letters, THANK YOU, Bibi IS AT THE DESK AND MISSES YOU ALL, COME TO THE big top WHEN YOU HEAR THE CALL, and sets the note on the tray; the small supply hoist rises like a held breath, and a moment later, somewhere above, there is a clatter of pure joy, pans rung like churchbells; message delivered'>>

choices.label: Go find the cook and seal the promise properly.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=102 beats='the cook ties on a fresh apron with the snap of a man who has been waiting forty years for exactly this; write the scene general, whichever memories stirred tonight, so it reads the same on any path; he begins prepping the centennial spectacular feast in earnest, seven courses and star buns and the frosted centennial cake's hundred candles counted twice, and his orders to Nia are simple: ready the hall, then call dinner, and no cook in this world or the next will be late'>>

choices.label: Head back to the grand staircase landing.
Go straight to the big top and begin.
Stay and taste every course as he builds it.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=ending words=96 beats='the jar comes willingly but its shelf-mates come too, a slow tipping avalanche of forty-year-old plum jam that Nia catches, juggles, and mostly wears; nothing breaks but her dignity; the cook appears at the crash, takes in the purple catastrophe, and laughs until the pots ring, then hands her a cloth and scrubs beside her, telling jam stories till the windows pale; she squelches home at dawn smelling of plums, with one rescued jar for breakfast and orders to come back hungry; the pantry keeps score, but kindly'>>

<<FILL role=ending words=96 beats='the small supply hoist accepts her weight with a doubtful creak and rises exactly one floor and a half before stopping dead between floors, Nia folded up like a letter in a very small envelope, more embarrassed than scared; unseen hands crank her gently back down, and the tray returns her to the kitchen with a new note in flour-dusted capitals, STAIRS EXIST; she laughs till her ears burn, promises the empty air to use them, and takes those stairs home at dawn, dignity in her pocket, grinning the whole way'>>

Go straight to the big top and begin.
Stay for the quilt fort midnight feast first.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=ending words=96 beats='the service corridors are a maze with a sense of humor; Nia pops out of the laundry-chute room, a shoe-shine nook, and a cupboard of two hundred lampshades, while invisible giggles conduct her along on a game of warmer-colder she is definitely losing; by the time she finds a window the sky is gray and her lantern is yawning; on her sleeve, discovered at the door, somebody has pinned a hand-drawn map of every shortcut on the floor, labeled FOR NEXT TIME, SO IT IS FAIR; a defeat, sportingly refereed'>>

<<FILL role=ending words=96 beats='they look at each other, and because Nia asked with her whole heart, they play it, point one thousand, in an empty corridor with no bell to start it; the towel-ball arcs, banks, drops, perfect, and lands with no sound at all, and everybody feels it at once, the point does not count, the corridor knows, the wall will not take the mark; by morning the tally reads 499 to 499 again, and the twins are relieved instead of sad, told you, party point; Nia heads home at dawn owing them an audience, and meaning to pay'>>

ending.title: The Point That Would Not Count

nodes.body: <<FILL role=ending words=98 beats='one more round becomes five becomes thirty; the corridor game has no natural ending and neither do the twins, and Nia plays until her arms are noodles and her laugh is cracked and the windows have gone traitorously pale; she surrenders on the carpet, honorable and destroyed, and the twins tuck her in the wing chair by the landing window like champions honoring the fallen; she wakes to her dad calling her name up the stairwell, one real peppermint on the chair arm, and the juggling score wall, she notices, now carries one small new column marked J'>>

<<FILL role=ending words=96 beats='the chute sighs warm air like a sleeping whale, and Nia's lantern lid clatters off down its throat, which means an expedition; three floors of spiral dark deliver her to the basement laundry, where clean sheets are folding themselves midair in stacks, unbothered by the century; they take one look at the tired child in their doorway and fold her in too, snug as an envelope, in the world's best-made bed; she wakes at dawn in room 31 with the door propped open and sun on the carpet, tucked in by experts, her lantern lid polished on the nightstand'>>

ending.title: The Laundry That Tucks You In

nodes.body: <<FILL role=ending words=98 beats='the quilt fort feast is crackers, forty-year peppermints in perfect condition, and secrets by lantern light: which doors stick, which floors sing, where the good echoes live, how to ride a trolley without squeaking the wheel; Nia trades them the outside world, buses, television, the existence of new peppermints, and their eyes go round as coins; the fort sails on till the windows pale, and she leaves knowing the hotel like a crewmate, sworn in with a sequin collection medal of her very own pinned over her heart'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=94 beats='the last stair narrows and climbs into wind-sound, walls curving close, and ends at a round door like a ship's hatch with a brass wheel; partway up, the stairwell wall is hung with little framed photographs climbing alongside the steps; keep this ascent general so it reads naturally on a first visit or any return; behind the hatch something vast and hollow hums with the weather'>>

choices.label: Climb slowly past the little framed photographs.
Take the low side door straight out to the roof walk.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=96 beats='the photographs are the same scene, year after year after year: five figures on this rooftop under summer stars, deck chairs in a row; the faces have faded to warm blurs but the shapes hold true, a tall one in cook's whites, a sun hat, two small saluting uniforms, and a small upright woman at the center of every single frame; the last photograph hangs beside an empty hook'>>

choices.label: Spin the brass wheel and enter the dome.
Step out onto the roof walk instead.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=100 beats='the observatory dome: a great brass telescope aimed through the half-open shutter at a slice of stars, star route charts papering the curved walls, and five stools of five heights in a tidy row, tallest to smallest to one with a cushion; the dome hums in the wind like a shell remembering the sea; keep this room general and re-entry-safe, the same waiting wonder on any visit'>>

choices.label: Look through the brass spyglass rig.
Study the star route charts on the curved wall.
Crank the stuck shutter wide open.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=94 beats='the eyepiece is warm, as if someone just stepped away, and through it the stars swim close enough to touch; tied to the focus knob is a small card, LAST AIMED AT THE PERSEIDS, LEAVE HER POINTED THERE FOR US, and beneath the words five sets of initials in five hands, P, O, W, M, M; a telescope kept like a promise, aimed at a patch of August sky'>>

choices.label: Leave it exactly as aimed, and look where it points.
Take the five initials to the star route charts.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='the eyepiece frames a modest patch of sky, unremarkable until you know it: the radiant of the Perseids, the meteor shower that returns every August like a family visit; the chart pinned nearest the scope counts the days in a childish tally, and Nia does the arithmetic twice with her heart speeding up, the shower is due back this very week; the sky has scheduled the party'>>

choices.label: Check the wall star route charts against the date.
Open the observation logbook of skies on the desk.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=96 beats='the star route charts are annotated in five hands taking turns: copperplate noting WISH MADE beside a meteor's path, grease pencil marking SUPPER AT MOONRISE, round green ink reminding that SEEDS WANT MOONLIGHT TOO, and two rival scrawls keeping meteor count down the margin, 51 against 51, tied, of course; one wall, one sky, one family all over it'>>

choices.label: Open the leather observation logbook of skies on the desk.
Look at the pencil marks climbing the doorframe.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=92 beats='the doorframe carries height marks in pencil, five columns of them, re-drawn year after year: two columns climbing fast and jostling each other, three holding steady and dignified, and every mark dated in the same careful hand; the last five marks all share one date, and after it the doorframe goes clean; a family measured itself here, right up until it stopped'>>

choices.label: Find that last date in the observation logbook of skies.
Crank the shutter wide and let the sky in.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=98 beats='the logbook of skies's last entry, forty years old, in copperplate: centennial spectacular TOMORROW, ALL FIVE OF US TOO EXCITED TO SLEEP, WATCHED AN EARLY METEOR AND MADE OUR WISH TOGETHER; WHATEVER HAPPENS, WE END EVERY GREAT EVENING ON THIS ROOF; Nia reads it twice and closes the book gently, like tucking it in; now she knows how a Star-Wagon evening is supposed to end'>>

choices.label: Carry that knowledge out to the roof walk.
Wind the little brass machine beside the logbook of skies.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=96 beats='the little brass machine is an miniature clockwork circus, but its sun is the Star-Wagon itself, a tiny hotel at the center, and its planets are five small figures that glide around it when wound: one circling the desk, one the mess wagon, one the garden, two chasing along the floors, and then, once each turn, all five meeting at the big top and rising together to the roof before beginning again; the family's whole year, in clockwork, still running true'>>

choices.label: Wind it again and again, learning the pattern by heart.
Take the pattern out to the roof walk.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=92 beats='the shutter crank grinds, groans, gives, and the dome's whole slice of sky yawns wide, stars pouring in; better, the dome itself rotates on its rail with a carousel-smooth glide when the crank turns, the heavens wheeling politely past the telescope; Nia, being eleven, immediately understands the possibilities of a room that spins'>>

choices.label: Crank harder and ride the spinning dome all the way around.
Steady it and look at the freed sky through the scope.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=98 beats='the roof walk: a wide boarded path behind a high safe rail, the town asleep far below, wind fresh off the dark; overhead looms the hotel's great unlit sign, THE Star-Wagon in a hundred cold bulbs, and along the boards the paint is worn in a curious pattern of scuffs; at the walk's end a small lookout cupola stands with its locker; keep the rooftop general and re-entry-safe on any visit'>>

choices.label: Find the sign's signal switch box.
Follow the worn scuff marks along the boards.
Walk to the lookout cupola at the end.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='the scuffs trace a repeating square with a little swing at each corner, and Nia has seen this diagram at school, it is a waltz, worn into the boards, danced here so many times the roof kept the steps; standing in the worn marks she can almost hear the tune arriving up the stairwells; they danced up here too, after; everything in this hotel ends in three-four time'>>

choices.label: Dance the square once, then walk to the turret.
Go find the sign's signal switch box.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=94 beats='the signal switch box is brass and serious, two switches inside: one furred with corrosion and labeled EVERY NIGHT, long dead, and one polished bright as new, labeled OCCASIONS, THE FAMILY WILL KNOW; forty years of dust everywhere except that one bright toggle, which somebody keeps ready; write this find general, reading the same on any visit; Nia's thumb hovers'>>

choices.label: Flip OCCASIONS, just to see.
Leave it ready and walk to the turret.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='one bulb answers, just one, the lamp at the center of the big M, warming to a soft ember-glow and holding there, patient, like a banked fire saying not yet, soon; and deep in the building beneath her feet Nia feels more than hears four faint far answers rise, a bell-note, a pot lid, a tap on tin, a double knock; the switch works; it is simply waiting, like everything here, for the real occasion; she eases it back with enormous respect'>>

choices.label: Walk on to the lookout cupola.
Head down to the staircase landing.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=92 beats='the lookout cupola's locker opens on party rigging: a line of a hundred little pennants rolled tight, a coil of halyard, and beneath them a steamer trunk with brass corners breathing out the smell of ginger and wool; the turret rail overlooks the whole dark roof walk and the hundred-bulb sign; a crow's nest waiting for a festival'>>

choices.label: String a short practice line of pennants.
Open the steamer trunk.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=94 beats='a dozen pennants run up the halyard and the wind snaps them bright and chattering against the stars, the first festival colors this roof has flown in forty years; and behind her, soft but unmistakable, comes a patter of applause with no hands in sight, warm as a lit window; Nia bows to the dark, which seems only polite, and the applause dissolves into wind that has clearly enjoyed itself'>>

choices.label: Open the steamer trunk next.
Rig the grand banner marked 100 as well, tonight.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=96 beats='the steamer trunk is the family's roof kit, packed by someone who loved them: five rolled deck chairs with name tags worn blank, two of them small; a biscuit tin, empty but still gingery; one blanket sized for five people who sit close; and on top, a brass conductor's whistle on a lanyard, its little tag reading SUPPER'S READY, THREE BLASTS; Nia holds forty years of family evenings in one chest and breathes the ginger smell'>>

choices.label: Try one soft blast on the brass conductor's whistle.
Set the five chairs in their worn dents and keep watch.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=94 beats='one soft blast, hardly more than a breath, and the whole building exhales beneath her: doors settle in their frames, pipes hum one warm note, and up from four far corners come the answers, a pot lid, a watering-can tap, two knocks in unison, and last, faint and silver, a bell; Nia laughs out loud, alone on a roof at midnight, because the Star-Wagon is a family that still answers supper calls, and she is the one holding the whistle'>>

choices.label: Hang the whistle ready and square away the roof.
Head down to the staircase landing.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=98 beats='Nia squares the roof away like a keeper: pennant line coiled to run free, whistle on its hook, signal switch box closed over its one bright toggle, chest latched but unlocked; the roof is ready to end a great evening the way this family always ended them; write this tidy-up general so it reads the same however tonight's climb unfolded; below her feet a hundred dark rooms wait for the party that starts everything'>>

choices.label: Head back down to the grand staircase landing.
Go straight down to the big top and begin.
Stay and write the after-party plan into the observation logbook of skies.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=ending words=94 beats='the dome takes Nia's enthusiasm and runs with it; the crank handle pops off in her hand mid-spin and the great shell carries on, two stately full laps of the heavens with Nia riding the rail like a very slow comet, before winding itself down with a satisfied clunk; taped inside the empty handle socket she finds a note in two rival scrawls, IT DOES THAT, and under it, SO DID WE; she reattaches the handle, dizzy and grinning, and takes the stairs down to bed while the stars stop revolving; the sky will hold its place until tomorrow'>>

<<FILL role=ending words=96 beats='the grand 100 banner is not a pennant, it is a sail, and the night wind has been waiting forty years for exactly this; the banner fills, hauls, and turns Nia into an anchor with her heels braced and her hat gone to the harbor, feet never leaving the boards but every button tested; she wrestles it down, folds it breathless, and lays it back in the locker, where it settles with the smugness of fabric that has made its point: the grand banner flies on the grand night, in company, with more hands; dawn is graying as she climbs down, windburned and wiser'>>

ending.title: The Banner Wants a Calmer Night

nodes.body: <<FILL role=ending words=96 beats='she sets the five chairs in their worn dents facing the Perseid patch, spreads the big blanket, and keeps the family's watch for them; the roof is quiet, the town sleeps, the stars wheel at their own pace; and near dawn one early meteor goes over, bright and brief, and Nia, sitting in the smallest chair, makes the family's wish for them since they cannot be here to make it; she will not say what it was, but walking home under a paling sky she is certain it is already coming true'>>

<<FILL role=ending words=96 beats='Nia winds the little brass hotel again and again until the pattern lives in her hands: desk, kitchen, garden, floors, and then everyone to the big top, everyone to the roof, around and around, a year of love with a crank on the side; by the time the windows pale she can close her eyes and walk the whole family through their whole day in her head; the party waits one more night, but Nia comes down the stairs carrying the Star-Wagon's heartbeat, and a keeper who knows the heartbeat can never really get lost'>>

<<FILL role=ending words=96 beats='she opens the logbook of skies to the first blank page in forty years and writes the plan in her best hand: chairs so, blanket so, whistle at moonrise, pennants up, one meteor minimum, everybody stays till the ginger tin is empty; the pen moves easy, as if the page had been saving itself; she signs it JUNE, NEWEST KEEPER, and under her name, in the morning, she will swear the ink added five small initials of its own; the logbook of skies goes back on the desk, pointed at the door, ready'>>

<<FILL role=choice words=122 beats='the big top's grand doors, tall as trees, carved with the number 100 in a wreath of gardenias; this is the room Bibi asked for, the evening that was never finished, and the proof is right here on site: through the crack between the doors Nia can see a banner sagging in the dark, sheeted tables still waiting, a ring of hanging lanterns hung with forty years of quiet; write this so it reads naturally whether the big top is Nia's first stop of the night or her last, whatever wings she has already wandered; the ring of hundred keys holds one stamped big top, warm in her fingers'>>

choices.label: Fit the big top key and unlock the grand doors.
Press an eye to the crack and look the long room over first.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='through the crack, the whole story stands frozen mid-party: THE Star-Wagon IS 100 on a banner, chairs angled toward a stage, a wind-up phonograph with its arm lifted as if interrupted between one note and the next; nothing spooky moves; it is a room that has been holding its breath politely for forty years, waiting for someone to come finish it; Nia's shiver turns into resolve'>>

choices.label: Unlock the grand doors.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='the big top key turns like it was oiled yesterday, and the doors swing wide without a creak, as if they had been practicing; lantern light rolls down a hall vast as a ship, over parquet dim with dust and mirrors gone cloudy; the air smells faintly of candle wax and gardenias, kept safe all this time; the room seems to straighten up, hopeful'>>

choices.label: Step inside and size up what a finished evening needs.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=104 beats='Nia stands in the middle of the great floor and thinks like a keeper: a party needs light, music, and a table; the ring of hanging lanterns hangs dark in its canvas with candle boxes stacked by the winch; the little stage holds a wind-up phonograph and a record cabinet; the long banquet table sleeps under dust sheets by the windows; keep this survey general, whichever corners already glow from earlier work, so it reads the same on a first pass or a return trip'>>

choices.label: See to the ring of hanging lanterns and its hundred candles.
See to the wind-up phonograph on the little stage.
See to the long banquet table.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='the ring of hanging lanterns winch wakes with a groan and lets the great crystal ring float down to Nia's height; the candle boxes hold one hundred pale candles, wrapped in paper like presents; she works steadily, setting each candle in its cup, and the crystals chime softly as if counting along with her'>>

choices.label: Hoist the ring of hanging lanterns home and light the hundred candles.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='the winch carries the blazing ring back up to the ceiling and the big top catches fire with gold, candlelight doubled in the cloudy mirrors and tripled in the polished floor where Nia's footprints have swept lanes through the dust; shadows scurry to the corners and settle there like well-behaved guests; the room is waking'>>

choices.label: Step back and see what the evening still needs.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='on the stage the wind-up phonograph's brass horn is dusty but whole, and in the record cabinet one sleeve stands apart from the rest, marked in careful copperplate, Bibi'S WALTZ, FOR THE centennial spectacular; Nia winds the crank slow and steady, the way you wind something precious, and the turntable begins to breathe'>>

choices.label: Set the needle down and test one bar.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='one bar of the waltz swells out of the horn and the whole room leans toward it; dust lifts off the chairs like held breath let go; somewhere far off in the hotel something answers, a soft thump, a clink, a patter, then hush; Nia lifts the needle gently, saving the song for the moment it belongs to'>>

choices.label: Step back and see what the evening still needs.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='the dust sheets come off the long table like sails coming down, and underneath the linen is still crisp and white, set and abandoned in the same hour forty years ago; at the head of the table five places stand slightly apart from the rest, the family's seats, kept first before any guest, just as Bibi said there were five'>>

choices.label: Set the five family places properly.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='Nia sets the five places with the best of everything the sideboard offers, candlesticks, folded napkins, polished silver; rather than guess which of the five belongs at which seat, she writes one card in her best letters for all of them together and stands it at the center, THE FAMILY, Star-Wagon 100; the five chairs seem to square their shoulders'>>

choices.label: Step back and see what the evening still needs.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=98 beats='Nia turns a slow circle in the great hall; corner by corner the big top is coming back to life, and the parts still waiting look eager rather than sad; write this moment general, whichever task she has just finished, one more corner glowing than before; the question now is whether the room wants more hands or whether it is time to call the family home'>>

choices.label: See to another corner of the hall.
It is ready enough. Sound the call.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=106 beats='by the service door Nia finds the hotel's three voices, kept side by side for occasions: a great bronze brass dinner gong with its mallet on a velvet hook, a braided pull-cord signal wire labeled FRONT DESK that runs away into the wall, and the wind-up phonograph waiting on stage with the doors of the big top standing open to the dark hotel; three honest ways to call a faded family to one bright room; Nia weighs them with her heart thudding'>>

choices.label: Strike the great brass dinner gong.
Pull the pull-cord signal wire that rings at Bibi's desk.
Let the waltz play with every door open.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='the gong's bronze note rolls out of the big top and down every pipe and shaft and stairwell like warm thunder; the building carries it gladly to its farthest floors; and back through the same pipes come answers, a clang of pot lids, a tap on a self-watering can, a double knock, a whistle down a stair; the Star-Wagon's family has heard dinner called'>>

choices.label: Stand by the open doors and watch the dark.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='Nia pulls the braided wire and far below the silver bell at the front desk begins to ring, and then, strangely and wonderfully, it keeps ringing on its own, ring for ring, as if a practiced hand has taken over; the sound multiplies up the stairwells until the whole hotel is one soft carillon saying the same word, home, home, home'>>

choices.label: Stand by the open doors and watch the dark.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='Nia sets the needle down and lets Bibi'S WALTZ pour out of the horn, through the flung-wide doors, into the dark halls like lamplight spilling; the hotel becomes an instrument around the song, banisters humming, ring of hanging lanterns chiming the melody back; no one who ever loved this tune could sleep through it, however faded they have grown'>>

choices.label: Stand by the open doors and watch the dark.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=126 beats='and they come; a warmth of bread and thyme arrives ahead of a broad cook with his sleeves rolled, wiping his hands on a fresh apron; a smell of rain and leaves arrives with a woman in a sun hat, a jar of white buds against her hip; two bellhop caps come racing each other down the banister and land in a tumble of giggles at the door; write each arrival so it reads warmly whether Nia already met them in tonight's wandering or is seeing them for the very first time, strangers to her or friends, they are plainly, achingly, a family arriving at a party; yet all four hesitate at the threshold, looking toward the one empty place at the head of the table'>>

choices.label: Run down and bring Bibi from her desk.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='Nia takes the lobby stairs two at a time; Bibi is at her desk, straightening the register like always, but her lamp is trembling; your family is in the big top, Nia says, and they are waiting for you; Bibi closes the book, picks up her silver bell, smooths her collar with see-through hands, and steps out from behind the desk for the first time in forty years; every lamp in the lobby rises to meet her'>>

choices.label: Walk her up to the big top doors.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=122 beats='at the doors, recognition returns the way candles catch, one wick lighting the next; the cook's face crumples and clears, Bibi, he says, I am Leon, I never stopped keeping your supper warm; the gardener laughs through tears, Marisol, she says, I grew your buttonholes every year; the twins collide into Bibi's skirts shouting Coco and Jojo and both at once; and Bibi says every name back like setting down heavy luggage at last; as they remember one another the fading runs backward, color climbing into their edges, five ghosts growing bright as lanterns; Nia stands in the doorway of the reunion she built'>>

choices.label: Let the evening finally begin.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=118 beats='the centennial spectacular, forty years late and not one bit spoiled; the waltz plays and Bibi dances with each of her family in turn; a feast arrives course by course as if it had been kept warm all this time; gardenias appear at every buttonhole, Nia's too; there are corridor games that spill in and out of the doorway, and a frosted centennial cake with a hundred candles that light themselves when the family laughs; Nia is passed from dance to dance like a guest of honor, which, everyone agrees, she is; the clock across the hall begins to gather itself toward midnight'>>

choices.label: Meet the family at the clock as it readies to strike twelve.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=124 beats='at the first stroke of midnight the five stand together glowing their brightest, and Bibi takes Nia's hands in her cool bright ones; the evening is finished at last, she says, and a finished evening frees a family to choose; we can rest now, all together, the way we always meant to end a great night, or we can stay and keep the Star-Wagon with your family as it wakes again; and because you gave us back to each other, newest keeper of the hundred keys, we would like you to help us choose; the clock strikes on, unhurried, while Nia looks from face to bright face'>>

choices.label: Wish them rest, and one sweet goodnight all together.
Ask them to stay and help wake the Star-Wagon.
Say the choice belongs to each of them alone.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=104 beats='Bibi nods as if Nia has passed a final, gentle test; then we end it properly, she says, we always finish a great evening on the roof, under the stars, all of us; the family climbs the hotel together, Nia in the middle of them, up through the sleeping floors to the rooftop where the night is clear and enormous and the deck chairs wait in their old worn dents facing the meteor sky'>>

choices.label: Settle under the blanket and watch for the first falling star.
Dance one last waltz on the roof walk first.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=102 beats='the twins cheer so hard their caps fall off, Leon is already planning breakfast menus, and Marisol declares the menagerie wagon will need a bigger gate for guests; Bibi rings her silver bell once, softly, to make it official; the Star-Wagon will reopen with two families keeping it, one you can see through and one you cannot; there is only one question left, and everyone looks at Nia to settle it'>>

choices.label: Keep the ghost family the hotel's warm secret.
Reopen as exactly what you are, the friendliest haunted hotel.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=106 beats='Bibi smiles the way grandmothers smile when a child says something older than its years; then each heart chooses its own door, she agrees; the family joins hands in the candlelight, Nia's hands folded into the circle too, and they decide to speak their wishes somewhere fitting; the twins vote loudly for the roof and the stars; Bibi looks toward the top floor, where a door-shaped outline waits at the end of the ninth-floor corridor, the hundredth door that never was'>>

choices.label: Climb to the roof and hear each wish under the sky.
Walk together to the hundredth door and whisper the wishes there.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=ending words=112 beats='they watch the sky in a row of creaking deck chairs, one blanket over ten knees, and when the first meteor crosses the Perseid patch the family brightens with it, laughing, waving to Nia, growing lighter and gladder until they are the same silver as the starlight, resting at last, together, mid-joy; the hotel below them exhales forty years of held breath; Nia walks down through a building that now feels like a blessing instead of a mystery, and every August after, when the meteors return, the Star-Wagon's windows seem to shine a little on their own, like family visiting'>>

<<FILL role=ending words=110 beats='the wind-up phonograph's song drifts all the way up the stairwells, and on the roof walk the family dances, Bibi with Leon, Marisol with both twins at once, and every one of them with Nia in turn, her sneakers finding the waltz worn into the boards; at the final turn of the final bar they bow to her, all five, and fade the way candle flames settle when the wick is done and the room is warm; morning finds the big top bright, the hundred keys polished on their ring, and one white gardenia laid on the front desk beside a silver bell at rest'>>

ending.title: The Last Waltz of the Star-Wagon

nodes.body: <<FILL role=ending words=108 beats='the Star-Wagon reopens in Nia, banner and brass band, and becomes gently famous for things nobody can quite explain: luggage that arrives before the guest does, suppers kept perfectly warm, gardenias that appear on bad days exactly when needed, a front desk where late arrivals always find one lamp lit; the guests say the hotel feels like being remembered; only Nia's family knows why, and on quiet nights Nia does her homework at the front desk while a silver bell rings itself, softly, just to keep her company'>>

<<FILL role=ending words=112 beats='they reopen as exactly what they are, THE HUNDRED-DOOR HOTEL, FAMILY RUN FOR ONE HUNDRED AND FORTY YEARS, SOME STAFF SEE-THROUGH, ALL STAFF KIND; children write letters asking if the twins will race them down the corridor, and the twins always win but carry the loser's bag like champions; Leon's dining room needs booking a season ahead; Marisol teaches a gardening club under glass; Bibi greets every guest by name on the second visit; and at the end of the ninth-floor corridor a hundredth door stands where the outline used to be, marked FAMILY, always warm to the touch'>>

<<FILL role=ending words=110 beats='under the meteor sky each of them speaks; the twins choose to stay, being nowhere near done playing; Leon stays, because a kitchen is never closed; Marisol will winter with her garden and summer with the stars, half and half, like a bulb; and Bibi chooses rest, but rest the way grandmothers rest, nearby, with the door ajar; none of it is goodbye, she tells Nia, family is a door that stays on the ring; when the Star-Wagon reopens, its keys hang bright at the desk, and five of them are always polished brightest'>>

<<FILL role=ending words=114 beats='at the end of the ninth-floor corridor they whisper, one by one, into the little keyhole in the door-shaped outline, and under Nia's hand the hundredth door becomes real, swings wide, and opens on their own best evening, kept the way the hotel keeps everything, warm and ready: the big top golden, the waltz mid-turn, the frosted centennial cake mid-laugh; now whenever any of them wishes, to visit, to rest a while, to come back and dance, the door is there, and Nia holds its key; the Star-Wagon reopens that summer with a hundred doors for guests and one door, the best one, for family'>>
